name: ScrollCapture API (View/Window scroll capture)
platform: Android platform API; introduced in Android 12 / API 31
license: [uncertain] Android platform API; AOSP source is Apache 2.0, but the runtime API is distributed as part of Android
version: [uncertain] Current Android platform API (docs current as of 2026)
project_status: [uncertain] Actively maintained in the Android platform and used by the system screenshot flow for long screenshots
capture_driver: System-driven long-screenshot capture. Android searches scrollable targets in the app window, requests tiles or regions from the chosen target, and assembles the result natively
compose_support: Not directly for classic Views; Compose support is separate and depends on Compose UI integration on API 31+
view_support: Scrollable Views with platform or custom scroll-capture callbacks; common candidates include ScrollView, ListView, RecyclerView, NestedScrollView, and WebView
stitching_strategy: Native tile-based incremental capture assembled by the screenshot host; no manual bitmap stitching by the app
matching_algorithm: Coordinate and tile placement, not computer-vision matching
scroll_offset_trust: Framework-managed via scrollBounds, captureArea, and session coordinates
duplicate_frame_handling: Not frame-diff based; the system requests only the needed regions or tiles
seam_selection_policy: Exact tile placement in content coordinates; typically no blending seam is needed
confidence_fallback: If a target reports empty or unavailable bounds it can be excluded; the system also ranks multiple targets and chooses the best candidate
static_chrome_handling: Scroll bounds are defined to include only content that moves with scrolling, which helps exclude toolbars, sticky chrome, and other non-scrolling regions
dynamic_content_robustness: Better than manual stitching because capture is content-coordinate based, but live-updating content can still change between requested tiles
content_type_fitness: Strong fit for long articles, lists, chats, and web content when the view cooperates with scroll capture
memory_strategy: Tile-based incremental capture using Surface/ImageReader in the system screenshot pipeline rather than one huge offscreen bitmap
stitching_speed: Usually fast because it avoids CV stitching and uses bounded tiles
max_output_constraints: [uncertain] SystemUI implementations use bounded tile buffers and practical height limits; exact OEM/export limits vary
output_format: Tiles or bitmaps internally; final image file is chosen by the screenshot host
output_quality: Loss-minimal native rendering of content; final export compression is host/OEM dependent
oem_compatibility: [uncertain] Availability and UX depend on OEM screenshot UI and whether the OEM keeps Android scroll-capture behavior
integration: Platform View/Window APIs such as View.setScrollCaptureHint, View.setScrollCaptureCallback, Window.registerScrollCaptureCallback, and ScrollCaptureSession
media_projection_compliance: Not applicable; this API does not use MediaProjection
windowing_behavior: Operates inside the target app window and selected scroll container rather than capturing the whole display
permissions_required: No special dangerous permission for an app merely supporting scroll capture
chart_stitching_fitness: High for long charts rendered inside a cooperative scrollable view, because capture is coordinate-based and does not rely on visual seam matching
chat_stitching_fitness: High for long chat threads in a cooperative scrollable view, because the system captures content regions directly
